item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+Open.
-Close.

at best, you could say the document alleges that the Foundation is attempting to reestablish containment procedures.

That is correct. I do say that the Foundation is attempting to reestablish containment. That is currently the way they are attempting to contain it.

There is no attempt to monitor or restrict access to the site.

I don't see any reason why someone would need to restrict access to SCP-5120 due to the fact it is already hidden from the public.

There are no indications of who is authorized to negotiate a new treaty or declare the old one satisfactorily reinstated.

This information would add nothing to the article in my opinion, and would end up coming off as needless fluff.

There are no instructions for what terms a negotiator ought to accept or reject.

Because as I stated previously… They are not currently negotiating with the SCP. Why would the people in charge of the SCP need to know this?

This article tells an in-world reader absolutely nothing about how the SCP is contained.

That's because its not.

it's astonishing in a Safe class SCP, and it would immediately tip off any reader that the article is a fake.

That's foreshadowing.

I understand you didn't like the article, which is fine, but the points you have proposed don't make very much sense to me, and I will also add that the entirety of the containment procedures you are talking about are indeed fake. The real containment procedures are at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The 4/XXXX SCP literally doesn't have containment procedures; at best, you could say the document alleges that the Foundation is attempting to reestablish containment procedures. There is no attempt to monitor or restrict access to the site. There are no indications of who is authorized to negotiate a new treaty or declare the old one satisfactorily reinstated. There are no instructions for what terms a negotiator ought to accept or reject. This article tells an in-world reader absolutely nothing about how the SCP is contained. It does tell us, however, that sapient entities from within the SCP know how to contact the outside world through couriers, and have even done so!

This absence of containment procedures should be utterly unacceptable for any SCP comprised of sapient entities, but it's astonishing in a Safe class SCP, and it would immediately tip off any reader that the article is a fake. Very firm downvote, for this and other reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have mixed feelings towards this…you pursued a very ambitious concept for a first SCP, and I really commend that. Even going for listpages and stuff.

The dialogue is pretty good. I don't really have qualms with that. The implications of an Eldritch being that slaughtered the mother-protector and has everyone imprisoned and essentially half-rotting under its thumb is an absolutely astounding concept. But…I don't know. I really don't know what it is that's nagging at me. I didn't like how 0-100 it went. It was felt too…sudden. Like you had two interviews, a bunch of emails, decided it was enough and that it was finally time to just go ham, and I really didn't like the way it was pulled off. Felt clumsy and sudden.

I also didn't buy the last audio log. Had zero punch whatsoever for me. A very generic, 'you sick fucks got me into this!' finality that didn't punch me like it should have.

No-vote. Apologies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Latin in this article is near incoherent. I tried to do a line-by-line crit of it but I wasn't able to get through more than two lines because of the amount of errors. There's also many more issues, like the fact this tries to pass off the Pantheon as "The Temple of Mars". I get there's only so many Roman buildings in the world and anything you have a photo of in an SCP is a real life thing by necessity but… you could choose a less famous building.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe you could have gotten to the twist faster, but you provided good addenda in my opinion.
For your first skip this is really good. I might not be very good with "the church of the broken god" and articles that have a link to others etc. but I definitely think this is underrated.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with Uranium that they are a few extra things in this article that don't need to be here. I think the logs of the city artifacts can be cut or shortened. The article could be focused on these entity creatures and little more foreshadowing and exploration of these people being messed up.

Since she lives there, there could be logs on what she sees and determines to be weir dor unnatural.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article tries carrying too many things at once, and ends up feeling incredibly disconnected as a result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it, but I have a question. In the end of the interview logs, when they refer to the library they use The Library. Are they referring to The Library or the library of the Fidelus Roma?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have one problem with this article: Cognitohazardous should be one word.

Otherwise, EXCELLENT article. Pacing was good, twist didn't feel too sudden as the buildup was nice and steady. Tone was phenomenal.

Upvoted with glee!

item.title: Oh shit
item.content:encoded:
This article went from 10 to 100. I guess the ending implies the people of Fidelis Roma are under the control of a malevolent deity, which killed the Mother.

I also found an SCP-XXXX you forgot:

The fucking thing that killed SCP-XXXX-1 is still here.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Alright, to start things, A BIG thanks to Grigori Karpin for just being a huge help during the entire process of making this article. You're the best!

Now to thank the people who helped me during the idea critique stage.

KaraKatt
Truc Linh
DolorousDoctor
ghosterposter
caspian2
Marcelles_Raynes
Francis Scalia
Grigori Karpin

And now the drafting stage.

Truc Linh
DrDromeus
DrSekora
Agent Roger Smith
Francis Scalia
Grigori Karpin

And finally thank you for reading my article.
